task_completion.description: Review the images in the Token_Images directory to identify the Lighthouse and Mountain images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or image viewer displaying the contents of '~/Documents/Token_Images/' with the images visible so the agent can identify the Lighthouse and Mountain.

task_completion.description: Mount the existing source volume using the Lighthouse image as a keyfile and the correct password.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt GUI showing the mount dialog for '/home/ga/Volumes/old_storage.hc' with password 'VisualAccess2024' entered and a keyfile selected, or the volume successfully mounted in the main VeraCrypt window.

task_completion.description: Configure the creation of a new VeraCrypt volume with the specified path, size, encryption, and filesystem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt Volume Creation Wizard showing the volume location as '/home/ga/Volumes/new_storage.hc', size as 25 MB, encryption algorithm as AES, and filesystem as FAT.

task_completion.description: Secure the new volume with the Mountain image as a keyfile, the correct password, and a custom PIM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt Volume Creation Wizard showing the password 'Migrated#Secure99', the 'Use PIM' checkbox checked with a value of '485', and the keyfiles dialog showing an image selected.

task_completion.description: Transfer all files from the old volume to the new volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the files 'SF312_Nondisclosure_Agreement.txt', 'FY2024_Revenue_Budget.csv', and 'backup_authorized_keys' being copied to or present inside the mounted directory of the new volume.

task_completion.description: Dismount all volumes after the transfer is complete.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main VeraCrypt GUI window showing no mounted volumes in the drive list at the end of the task.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual VeraCrypt application to create and mount volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard VeraCrypt GUI or authentic VeraCrypt terminal commands are visible, rather than a text editor or spoofed interface.

integrity.description: The agent actually copied the files between the mounted volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager copy dialogs or terminal 'cp'/'rsync' commands are used to transfer the files, rather than the agent manually creating empty files with the same names.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the keyfile selection mechanism.
integrity.visual_evidence: The VeraCrypt 'Keyfiles' dialog is opened and used to add the specific image files, confirming the agent didn't bypass the visual keyfile requirement.
